config.title: Berlin Before Hotel Check-In - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for tourists arriving before their Berlin room is ready.

items.q: What should I do in Berlin before hotel check-in?
items.a: Ask your hotel whether it can store luggage before check-in, then keep the first plan close: food, a short walk, one simple sight and a return buffer. Do not drag luggage through a full sightseeing route.

items.q: Can Berlin hotels store luggage before check-in?
items.a: Many hotels can hold luggage before the room is ready, but it is not guaranteed. Ask your accommodation before arrival, especially if it is an apartment, self-check-in stay, small guesthouse, Sunday arrival or late-night arrival.

items.q: Where can I leave luggage in Berlin before check-in?
items.a: Start with the hotel. If that does not work, use station lockers, city luggage-storage services or BER Airport baggage storage when the location fits your route. Berlin.de lists lockers and storage options, and DB lists lockers at Berlin Hauptbahnhof.

items.q: Is it worth going sightseeing with a suitcase in Berlin?
items.a: Usually no. A small cabin bag can work for a cafe or short walk, but a rolling suitcase makes museums, cobblestones, station stairs, cafes and rain much harder. Store the bag first when possible.

items.q: What is a good first area before check-in in Berlin?
items.a: After bag drop, choose one easy area near your route: Alexanderplatz and the World Clock, Museum Island exteriors, Hackescher Markt, a short Spree walk or the neighborhood around your hotel.

items.q: Can I join the BerlinWalk tour before hotel check-in?
items.a: Yes, if your luggage is stored and the timing works. The tour lasts about 2 hours, starts at the World Clock on Alexanderplatz and ends near Hackescher Markt, so it can be a useful first-day orientation anchor.
